title: Synchaeta silvatica
taxonomy: Synchaeta silvatica is a species of rotifer, which is a type of microcrustacean in the phylum Rotifera. Rotifers are small, usually microscopic, invertebrate animals that are found in aquatic environments worldwide. They are typically free-swimming and feed on small organisms such as algae, bacteria, and other tiny invertebrates. As a member of the phylum Rotifera, Synchaeta silvatica is also classified within the kingdom Animalia. This classification indicates that Synchaeta silvatica is a multicellular, eukaryotic organism with a complex body structure and no backbone.
